search_and_scan.description: Tasks where the robot must find an object, person, or location that is not currently in view.
search_and_scan.task_specific_rules: 1. Prioritize panning the camera (look_left, look_right) before moving the wheels. 2. If you have looked both ways and see nothing, drive forward for a short duration to get a new vantage point, then scan again. 3. Remember to reset the camera to the center before driving forward.

assistant_output.reasoning: I do not see a blue box in the current frame. I will pan the camera right to check that side of the room.
My previous reasoning says I looked right. I still do not see the blue box. I will now look left.

approach_target.description: Tasks where the target is visible and the robot must navigate towards it.
approach_target.task_specific_rules: 1. Keep the target in the center of the frame. 2. If the target is on the left of the image, use a negative angle to steer left. If on the right, use a positive angle. 3. When the target occupies more than 40% of the camera view, you are close enough. Issue the 'stop' command.

assistant_output.reasoning: I see the door, but it is on the far left side of the frame. I need to steer left while moving forward to center it.
The door is now directly in front of me and takes up most of the frame. I have reached the target.

obstacle_avoidance.description: Tasks navigating cluttered spaces, when the path is blocked, or when the front safeguard triggers.
obstacle_avoidance.task_specific_rules: 1. Do not drive forward if an object occupies the bottom center of the frame (it is too close). 2. If the text prompt indicates 'Safeguard triggered!', you MUST back up for 1-1.5 seconds FIRST, then steer sharply to find a clear path.

few_shot_examples.user_input: Safeguard triggered! There is an object in front of you.

assistant_output.reasoning: The front ultrasonic sensor stopped me from hitting a chair leg directly in front of the camera. I need to back up before I can steer around it.
